
| Code System | Code(s) | Description |
|---|---|---|
| SNOMED CT | `56717001` | Tuberculosis (disorder) |
| ICD-10-CM | `A15` | Respiratory tuberculosis |
| ICD-10-CM | `A16` | Respiratory TB (unconfirmed) |
| ICD-10-CM | `A17` | TB of nervous system |

### Notes

- Add `&clinical-status=active` to any query to filter for **currently active** TB diagnoses only.

## FHIR Queries — TB Patient Identification (Two-Resource Union Strategy)

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|56717001,http://snomed.info/sct|68235000

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9384,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|6038
```

---

### Query Breakdown

#### Query 1 — Condition (SNOMED CT TB Diagnoses)

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| `code` | `snomed\|56717001` | **Tuberculosis (disorder)** — broader umbrella concept; used when site/type is unspecified |
| `code` | `snomed\|68235000` | **Pulmonary tuberculosis** — most prevalent subtype; often coded separately for specificity |

> The comma between codes applies **OR logic** within a single parameter, returning Conditions matching either concept in one server round-trip.

---

#### Query 2 — MedicationRequest (RxNorm Anti-TB Drugs)

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| `code` | `rxnorm\|9384` | **Rifampin** (ingredient) — cornerstone of active TB treatment and LTBI prophylaxis |
| `code` | `rxnorm\|6038` | **Isoniazid** (ingredient) — used in both RIPE regimens and 9-month monotherapy for latent TB |

> Captures patients receiving empirical/prophylactic anti-TB therapy **before** microbiologic confirmation and those who never receive a coded Condition (e.g., high-risk contacts on INH alone).

---

### Union Logic (Post-Query)

```
TB Patient Set = DISTINCT( Condition.subject refs ) ∪ DISTINCT( MedicationRequest.subject refs )
```

- Extract `subject.reference` (Patient IDs) from each result set
- Deduplicate within each set, then union across both sets
- Patients appearing in **both** sets (diagnosed *and* treated) are counted once

---

### Design Notes

- **Why not one query?** FHIR has no native cross-resource OR; the union must be assembled client-side
- **Coverage gap addressed:** Patients empirically treated or on contact prophylaxis lack ICD/SNOMED codes — the medication path closes that gap
- Add `&_elements=subject` to each query to return only patient references and minimize payload
- Add `&clinical-status=active` to the Condition query if you want to exclude resolved/historical TB diagnoses

### Query Breakdown

| Query | Drug | RxNorm CUI | Role in TB Regimen |
|---|---|---|---|
| Query 1 | **Rifampin** (Rifampicin) | `9384` | Bactericidal; cornerstone of TB treatment & prophylaxis |
| Query 2 | **Isoniazid** (INH) | `6038` | Bactericidal; also used alone for LTBI prophylaxis |

---

### Design Rationale

| Decision | Justification |
|---|---|
| **Ingredient-level RxNorm codes** | CUIs `9384` and `6038` are at the **ingredient** tier of the RxNorm hierarchy, enabling hits across all dose forms, strengths, and brand names (e.g., Rifadin, Nydrazid) |
| **Two separate queries** | Medications may be ordered as **separate prescriptions** (standard practice) rather than as a co-formulated product |
| **`_include=MedicationRequest:subject`** | Pulls associated `Patient` resources in a single round-trip for patient identification |
| **`_include=MedicationRequest:medication`** | Resolves external `Medication` references when `medicationReference` is used instead of `medicationCodeableConcept` |
| **Coverage of combination products** | Systems implementing RxNorm normalization will also surface: **Rifamate** (rifampin 150mg + isoniazid 300mg) and **Rifater** (rifampin + isoniazid + pyrazinamide) via their ingredient codes |

---

### Clinical Population Captured

```
Full Treatment Course (RIPE)    ──► rifampin + isoniazid orders present
LTBI / Prophylaxis              ──► isoniazid-only (Query 2) or 3HP/4R regimens
Contact Prophylaxis             ──► isoniazid monotherapy or rifampin monotherapy
Empirical TB Treatment          ──► both orders present without coded diagnosis
Meningococcal Prophylaxis       ──► rifampin-only (Query 1), no isoniazid match
```

> **Intersection logic**: Patients appearing in **both** result sets (subject references matching across Query 1 and Query 2) represent the highest-specificity TB treatment cohort, filtering out rifampin-only meningococcal prophylaxis cases.
